title: The Great Mosque of Isfahan
topic_path: Art of the Islamic world 640 to now

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Great Mosque of isfahan is unique because it was made during a single building campaign and not over a long period of time.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f6785d7f57c26112eb670118b5d976dd.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The Great Mosque of Isfahan is the sum of building and decorating activities carried out from the 8th through the 20th centuries.**

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/west-and-central-asia/a/medieval-period-the-great-mosque-or-masjid-e-jameh-of-isfahan)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Great Mosque of Isfahan is unusual because of its urban integration and once served as a pedestrian hub in the city.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f6785d7f57c26112eb670118b5d976dd.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Positioned at the center of the old city, the mosque shares walls with other buildings. Due to its immense size and its numerous entrances, it formed a pedestrian hub, connecting the arterial network of paths crisscrossing the city.** 

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/west-and-central-asia/a/medieval-period-the-great-mosque-or-masjid-e-jameh-of-isfahan)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT a feature of The Great Mosque of Isfahan?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f6785d7f57c26112eb670118b5d976dd.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The Great Mosque of Isfahan is decorated with many painted tiles, but not mosaic.**

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/west-and-central-asia/a/medieval-period-the-great-mosque-or-masjid-e-jameh-of-isfahan)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the following architectural feature with the correct image.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
**The iwan is a vaulted space that opens to a courtyard. Muqarnas are traditional Islamic cusped niches. The prayer hall looks toward the iwan.**
